compliance_patterns.description: Ministries follow formal government procurement regulations with strict evaluation criteria
Deliverables require formal ministerial review and approval chains

evaluator_insights.insight: Ministry evaluators are typically senior civil servants who value formality and protocol
evaluator_insights.implication: Proposal tone must be formal and demonstrate respect for institutional hierarchy
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Use formal language, include proper titles, and show understanding of ministerial governance structures

evaluator_insights.insight: Ministries prioritize alignment with national development agendas and policy objectives
evaluator_insights.implication: Proposals must explicitly connect deliverables to the ministry's policy mandate
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Map proposal outcomes to the ministry's stated strategic objectives and national priorities

evaluator_insights.insight: Ministry evaluation committees often include both technical and administrative members
evaluator_insights.implication: Technical proposals must be accessible to non-technical evaluators
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include executive summaries and visual aids that communicate technical approaches clearly

evaluator_insights.insight: Knowledge transfer and capacity building are critical evaluation criteria for ministries
evaluator_insights.implication: Proposals that only deliver reports without building internal capability score lower
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include dedicated knowledge transfer phase with training workshops and capability handover

forbidden_assumptions: Do NOT assume ministry decision-makers are purely technical — administrative and political considerations matter
Do NOT assume the ministry has no prior consulting experience — they may have strong opinions on methodology
Do NOT assume one ministry contact speaks for the entire institution — stakeholder mapping is essential
